cves.name: Log4Shell
cves.description: Found in December 2021 in Java's Log4j logging library. Practically the entire internet was affected — Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, Tesla and more.
cves.how_exploit: Attacker sends this string in any input field. Log4j automatically executes it while logging — downloads and runs code from attacker's server. Remote Code Execution.
cves.fix: Upgrade to Log4j version 2.17.1 or above. Disable JNDI lookups.

cves.name: Heartbleed
cves.description: Memory leak bug in the OpenSSL library. Attackers could read the server's private memory — passwords, private keys, session tokens — all exposed.
cves.vulnerable_code:
memcpy(bp, p, payload);
// payload size was never validated!
cves.how_exploit: Attacker sends a crafted heartbeat request with a larger payload size than actual data. Server returns extra memory contents without validation — leaking sensitive data.
cves.fix: Update OpenSSL to version 1.0.1g or higher.
cves.fixed_code:
if (payload + padding > s->s3->rrec.length)
    return 0; // Reject invalid request

cves.name: SQL Injection Classic
cves.description: Most common and dangerous vulnerability. Allows attackers to directly control and manipulate the database.
cves.vulnerable_code: query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name='" + username + "'"
cves.how_exploit:
Enter as username: ' OR '1'='1
Query becomes: SELECT * FROM users WHERE name='' OR '1'='1'
Result: Entire database is exposed!
cves.fix: Use parameterized queries — keep user input completely separate from the SQL query.

cves.name: IDOR - Broken Access Control
cves.description: OWASP #1 vulnerability in 2021. Changing the ID in a URL allows access to another user's data without authorization.
cves.vulnerable_code:
# URL: /profile?id=123
user = db.get_user(request.args.get('id'))
return user.data  # No authorization check!
cves.how_exploit: Change id=123 to id=124 in the URL — another user's profile is returned. There is no server-side authorization check.
cves.fix: Always perform server-side authorization checks on every request.
cves.fixed_code:
user = db.get_user(request.args.get('id'))
if user.id != current_user.id:
    return 403  # Forbidden
return user.data

cves.name: Broken Authentication
cves.description: Weak authentication mechanisms that allow attackers to hijack user accounts.
cves.vulnerable_code:
# Weak JWT secret
jwt.encode(payload, 'secret', algorithm='HS256')
# Password stored in plain text
db.save(password=user_password)
cves.how_exploit: Weak JWT secrets can be cracked using brute force. Plain text passwords are immediately exposed in any database leak.
cves.fix: Use strong secrets, always hash passwords, and implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).
cves.fixed_code:
import secrets
import bcrypt
jwt_secret = secrets.token_hex(32)
hashed = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt())
